calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeannette Fisher-Kouadio for (executor) Patricia Nelson.

The Court stated that there are a couple of issues, that the bond needed to be increased but was not and that the statutory fee calculation needs to take into account the losses. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported that after the sale the estate had 1.6 million in funds, that her client is 80 years old and on a fixed income, that the funds were placed into several bank accounts, that there is $37,000.00 in reserve for property taxes, that they currently have $13,500.00 in tax bills, that they currently have a bond for $10,000.00 and that they did not receive an order indicating the bond needed to be increased. The Court stated that it is counsel's responsibility to increase the bond if the administrator fails to do so. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the will waived bond, that they thought the bond was only needed for the 1.6 million and that they misunderstood the court's order regarding the bond. The Court stated that it did allow the 1.6 million to be placed into blocked accounts and that it is the reserve that needs to be bonded. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that she has already spoken with Philips bonding and will have the bond tomorrow. The Court stated that counsel can expedite the ex parte application for increase of bond. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio addressed the statutory fee amount stating that it has been the practice of this court that if there is a waiver of accounting the computation is based on the inventory & appraisal. The Court stated that that is not the practice of this court, that the statute needs to be followed, that the rules of court are very clear that you must calculate the gains and losses. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that she always attempts to give all of the information and that this is the exact opposite of what the other examiners have told her. The Court stated that it follows the statutes. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio cited 10954(c)(1). The Court cited 7.550(b). Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the language is a little loose. The Court stated that the language could not be clearer and that it will take the accounting petition under submission with the expectation of granting it with a reduced statutory fee and subject to the increased bond being taken care of.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeannette Fisher-Kouadio for (administrator/executor) Patricia Nelson.

The Court stated that there is no appearance by any family member to object. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the family is in agreement, that initially some of the heirs had been contacted by a service, that they have recently filed a release, that the decedent had filled out a pre-formatted will, that it was prepared late in life while ill, that he did not populate the beneficiary line, cited a statute that if a line is unclear or blank testacy laws are to be applied, that declarations have been filed attesting to the decedent's stated of mind and desire to have Ms. Nelson as sole beneficiary, that Courtney Bennett supports this and that there is no other opposition to it. The Court stated that no one is the beneficiary under this will and that the anti-lap statute needs to be applied. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that the anti-lap statute only applies if the beneficiaries survive the decedent by 45 days and that Ms. Bennett actually died prior to the decedent. The Court stated that 50% is to go to the living sister and 50% to the deceased sister's estate. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio cited caselaw and stated that the decedent wanted the entire estate to go to Ms. Nelson and that there are 5 declarations filed in support of this. The petition to determine distribution rights was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado: Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Receipt For Funds Into Blocked Accounts
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Bond For Full Amount Of Money In The Estate
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for (executor) Patricia Nelson.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported that the amount of bond is problematic for her client, that she does not support a bond of $1,200,000.00, suggested that they put the funds into blocked accounts and stated that her client will get the instructions to the bank tomorrow. The Court stated that it needs an order directing the deposit of all estate funds into blocked accounts, that multiple accounts would be necessary so that the funds would be secured, confirmed that Ms. Fisher-Kouadio had closing statements and requested that she file them with the court. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that there is actually $1,600,000.00 in the account and that the additional $400,000.00 consists of proceeds from the other property and accounts. The status on filing bond for full amount of money in the estate will be replaced by a status of filing receipts for funds into a blocked account and is set for August 4, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Continued To Aug-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for Patricia Nelson.

The Court stated that the current petition still needs proof of service, that we are not in a position to proceed and that there is concern that the client filed incorrect statements not acknowledging the sister. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that her client is 80 years old, that she thought that because the person was deceased she was not an heir to the estate and that during a conversation she spoke of a nephew and at that time contact information was requested. The Court reminded counsel that it should be standard procedure to ask about relatives. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that Ms. Nelson represented that she was her brother's only heir, that a friend of Mr. Winstead accused them of prolonging the process and was adamant that there was no other sister, that the properties have been sold, that except for distribution they are ready to close, that there is $1,200,000.00 in the estate and that the will waived bond however there is a small bond as Ms. Nelson is out of state. The Court stated that there needs to be security for that money, that the full amount needs to be bonded and asked if service has been made on the other heirs. Ms. Fisher-Kouadio stated that service has not been made and that it was an oversight. The petition to determine distribution rights was continued to August 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing bond for full amount of money in the estate is set for July 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Interpret Will
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Interpret Will
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Continued To May-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeannette Fisher-Kouadio for Patricia Nelson.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported that they have uncovered heirs that were not notified, that one heir is deceased but leaves children and a surviving spouse, that they are still missing one address for an heir, that the request in the petition will be that the decedent intended for Patricia Nelson to be the sole beneficiary, that there was a drafting error on the will and that she has 3 people who will attest to that. The status on filing a petition to interpret will was continued to May 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Interpret Will
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanette Fisher-Kouadio for (Representative) Patricia Nelson.

Ms. Fisher-Kouadio reported what the administration has been doing, that they have been extremely busy, that the executor has been unavailable and that they expect to file the petition in a week. The status on filing a petition to interpret will was continued to April 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
